message:
chore(beads): close s0v0, ud94, qcmh (stream parity)

Closed via direct JSONL edit (per memory feedback_br_sync_drift).
All three fixes shipped in 0bc2ee1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Note that for CREATE you may want to use the MKSTREAM option to create an empty stream automatically.'

Acceptance: byte-equal to upstream. Affects 'XGROUP CREATE noexist g 0' (without MKSTREAM); the MKSTREAM path already works.  {"id": "frankenredis-qhid", "title": "Implement metatables (getmetatable/setmetatable) for Lua 5.1 compatibility", "description": "# Lua Metatables

## What
Metatables are a key Lua 5.1 feature. Some Redis Lua scripts use them for operator overloading or custom table behavior. Currently missing from the hand-written interpreter.

## Why
While rare in typical Redis scripts, metatables are part of the Lua 5.1 spec. Their absence could break third-party Lua libraries that depend on them.

## Implementation
1. Add a metatable field to LuaTable
2. Implement getmetatable()/setmetatable() global functions
3. Implement __index, __newindex, __tostring, __eq, __lt, __le, __add, __sub, __mul, __div, __mod, __pow, __concat, __len, __call metamethods
4. Dispatch to metamethods when operations fail on the base table

## Scope
This is significant work (~200-400 lines). Prioritize __index and __tostring as they are most commonly used.

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:13:08.354875762Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:10:45.092683154Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:10:45.092404106Z", "close_reason": "Implemented getmetatable/setmetatable with real metatable storage (Option<LuaTable> in LuaTableInner). Added __index metamethod support via get_with_index() for table subscript, field access, and method calls. Supports chained __index (table→table). Added 5 tests covering get/set, __index fallback, chain, priority, and nil removal.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-qi6z", "title": "s5su part d: stream decoder extensions for LISTPACKS_2 (19) + LISTPACKS_3 (21) — consumer groups + seen/active times", "description": "Extend the s5su-c decoder to parse consumer-group + consumer + PEL state from upstream type-19 and type-21 stream records. Populates the groups vec on RdbValue::Stream.

Note on type tag collision: RDB_TYPE_HASH_WITH_TTLS is currently 21 (set in rv89/th7q, 25d6a64). This conflicts with upstream's RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 which is also 21. Options:
  * Option A: Shift our internal HashWithTtls to a reserved range (e.g., 100+) so upstream 21 can be our decoder-only stream path.
  * Option B: Keep our internal 21 for hash; upstream interop only ever reads types 15/19 (losing LISTPACKS_3-specific features when ingesting 21-tagged streams from real Redis).

Plan: adopt Option A. Revise th7q's type byte to 100, document the change, re-run th7q tests. Then use 21 exclusively for the stream decoder in upstream-produced files.

Blocks s5su-e.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:17:24.348682901Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T23:12:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T23:12:00Z", "close_reason": "live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream (m17o) exercises type-21 decoder in reverse direction (oracle DUMP → our restore_key) for streams with consumer-group-free entries. Entries-only path validated; LISTPACKS_2 consumer-group extensions can be added as needed when a test case drives them.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

+{"id": "frankenredis-s0v0", "title": "fr-command: parse_stream_id rejects bare-integer IDs (1) that upstream accepts as 1-0", "description": "fr-command::parse_stream_id at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:4829 requires 'ms-seq' form and rejects bare integers like '1' or '12345'. Upstream Redis 7.2 (t_stream.c::streamGenericParseIDOrReply) accepts the bare form: '1' → (ms=1, seq=missing_seq) where missing_seq defaults to 0 for most commands. This affects:
  - XSETID s 1 — fr returns 'Invalid stream ID', upstream returns OK
  - XACK nokey g 1 — fr returns 'Invalid stream ID', upstream returns 0
  - any other stream cmd with a bare-int ID

Acceptance: parse_stream_id accepts:
  - 'ms-seq' (current)
  - bare 'ms' (sets seq to caller-specified missing_seq, default 0)
  - '-' (ms=0, seq=0) when non-strict mode
  - '+' (ms=UINT64_MAX, seq=UINT64_MAX) when non-strict mode

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).
  XSETID s 1 — fr 'ERR Invalid stream ID...', redis 'OK'
  XACK nokey g 1 — fr 'ERR Invalid stream ID...', redis 0

Fix: enhance parse_stream_id to accept bare-int form. May need a new optional missing_seq parameter; default to 0 to match upstream's most common call site.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T03:18:59.960655248Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T03:51:42.570638Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T03:51:42.570638Z", "close_reason": "parse_stream_id now accepts bare-int form ('1' → (1, 0)) matching upstream t_stream.c::streamGenericParseIDOrReply. XACK/XSETID/etc now accept integer-only IDs. Verified against vendored redis 7.2.4. Shipped in 0bc2ee1."}
 {"id": "frankenredis-s10v", "title": "CLIENT NO-EVICT + CLIENT TRACKING (RESP3 invalidation push) parity audit", "description": "(a) CLIENT NO-EVICT ON|OFF: prevents the eviction policy from picking this client when maxmemory is enforced. Verify the flag is recorded in CLIENT INFO output and CLIENT LIST 'flags' field shows 'e' on (no-evict). Today our session_no_evict toggle is plumbed but the flags string in CLIENT INFO/LIST may not match upstream.

(b) CLIENT TRACKING ON [REDIRECT id] [BCAST] [PREFIX p ...] [OPTIN|OPTOUT] [NOLOOP]: turn on client-side caching. The server pushes invalidation frames to the tracking client (or the redirect target) when watched keys are mutated. Validate:
  * REDIRECT: invalidations go to the target client, not the requestor
  * BCAST + PREFIX: invalidations only fire for keys matching at least one prefix
  * OPTIN: only keys read after CLIENT CACHING YES are tracked
  * OPTOUT: all keys are tracked except those after CLIENT CACHING NO
  * NOLOOP: don't push invalidations back to the source of the mutation

(c) Invalidation framing: in RESP2 the message is `[invalidate, [keys]]` Array; in RESP3 it must be a Push frame ('>' prefix). See br-frankenredis-r80v for the framing layer.

(d) CLIENT TRACKINGINFO returns a map with redirect, flags, prefixes, redirect-target. Verify shape.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: needs multi-client harness (one tracker, one mutator).", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-25T04:00:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-26T05:30:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-26T05:30:00Z", "close_reason": "CLIENT INFO flags string parity (e/T/t/B/P/r/x) shipped via fr-runtime client_info_line_for_session. CLIENT TRACKING (REDIRECT/BCAST/PREFIX/OPTIN/OPTOUT/NOLOOP) wiring + TRACKINGINFO already in place. RESP3 invalidation push framing tracked separately under r80v.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["client", "invalidation", "no-evict", "resp3", "tracking"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-s11v", "title": "DEBUG SLEEP + DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE + DEBUG JMAP for test infra parity", "description": "Several DEBUG subcommands that conformance/integration tests rely on:

(a) DEBUG SLEEP <seconds>: blocks the connection for the specified time. Used by replication tests to simulate slow commands. Today we may either no-op or block the wrong way.

(b) DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0|1: turn off active expiry for tests that want to inspect lazily-expired keys. We accept the value but verify it actually disables the active expire cycle.

(c) DEBUG JMAP: returns a JSON dump of internal state. Optional but Redis Sentinel tests use it.

(d) DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN <pattern> <string>: bounded-iteration glob test. Useful for hardening proptest fuzz inputs.

(e) DEBUG CHANGE-REPL-ID: rotate replication-id. Used in replication recovery tests.

(f) DEBUG QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD: tune list quicklist node packing for tests.

(g) DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN: see (d).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_debug.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff. The vendored oracle needs --enable-debug-command yes (already configured).", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-25T04:00:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-26T00:30:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-26T00:30:00Z", "close_reason": "DEBUG CHANGE-REPL-ID + STRINGMATCH-LEN/TEST + QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD wired with upstream wording. SLEEP/SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE/JMAP already present. JMAP=accept-and-OK matches upstream OBJ wire contract.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["debug", "expire", "replication", "test-infra"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-s12v", "title": "FAILOVER command + replication checkpoint semantics", "description": "FAILOVER [TO host port [FORCE]] [ABORT] [TIMEOUT ms] is Redis 7.0+ replication failover orchestration. Validate:

(a) FAILOVER without TO: pauses writes, waits for replica sync, then promotes the most-up-to-date replica.

(b) FAILOVER TO host port: targets a specific replica. FORCE skips the wait-for-sync if the timeout elapses.

(c) FAILOVER ABORT: cancels an in-progress failover.

(d) FAILOVER TIMEOUT ms: bound the wait-for-sync window.

(e) Reply shape: +OK on success, -ERR on failure (cluster mode required, no replicas available, etc.).

(f) State machine: must transition through PAUSE_WRITES → SYNC → ROLE-SWAP atomically. CLIENT PAUSE WRITE prevents new writes during the window.

(g) Replication checkpoint: REPLCONF GETACK + WAITAOF interaction with FAILOVER.

Today our FAILOVER returns a generic error. Wire it to actually stop accepting writes and respond per upstream's state machine.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: needs multi-process master+replica harness.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-25T04:00:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-26T05:32:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-26T05:32:00Z", "close_reason": "FAILOVER cluster-mode pre-check shipped (rejects with upstream wording before every other validation). State-machine cluster-vs-replica precedence + ABORT + TIMEOUT + TO host port FORCE all wired with upstream wording per replication.c::failoverCommand. Real-replica state machine deferred to substantive replication work.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["cluster", "failover", "replication"]}

+{"id": "frankenredis-ud94", "title": "fr-command: NOGROUP error wording differs from upstream (XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM)", "description": "XPENDING / XAUTOCLAIM emit 'NOGROUP No such consumer group <g> for key name <k>' when the consumer group is missing. Upstream Redis 7.2 emits 'NOGROUP No such key <k> or consumer group <g>' (note the 'No such KEY ... or consumer group' wording, used for both missing-key and missing-group cases — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c).

Acceptance: error text byte-equal to upstream. Both 'XPENDING nokey g' and 'XAUTOCLAIM s g c 0 0-0' should emit the new wording. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). Fix: search-replace the old error wording in fr-command (likely a const).", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T03:19:08.273775918Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T03:51:42.570638Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T03:51:42.570638Z", "close_reason": "XPENDING/XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM/XREADGROUP NOGROUP errors now emit upstream's 'No such key X or consumer group Y' wording (with XREADGROUP suffix) byte-for-byte. XGROUP-internal SETID/DELCONSUMER/CREATECONSUMER preserve the 'for key name' form. Conformance fixture core_stream.json updated. Shipped in 0bc2ee1."}

 {"id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "title": "EPIC: Implement real cross-process replication (PSYNC full-resync + partial-resync + live streaming)", "description": "# Replication Epic

## Why This Is Critical
FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication, but this is false. The state machine logic (665 lines in fr-repl) is tested in isolation, but:
- REPLICAOF returns "OK" without actually connecting to a primary
- No bytes ever flow between two FrankenRedis processes
- Cannot replicate from a real Redis primary
- A real Redis replica cannot connect to FrankenRedis as primary
- Zero cross-process replication tests exist

The PSYNC decision logic, backlog window math, and handshake FSM are correct in isolation — the gap is the actual TCP client that connects, negotiates, receives snapshots, and streams commands.

## What Success Looks Like
1. `frankenredis --replicaof host port` connects to a primary (Redis or FrankenRedis), completes PSYNC handshake, receives RDB snapshot, applies it, and enters live streaming mode
2. A second FrankenRedis instance can connect as a replica to a FrankenRedis primary
3. Writes to the primary appear on the replica within bounded latency
4. Partial resync works after brief disconnection (backlog covers the gap)
5. An integration test demonstrates this end-to-end

## Architecture
The existing fr-repl state machine and fr-server replica sync code form the skeleton. The missing pieces are:
1. Real TCP client connection in REPLICAOF handler (currently a no-op)
2. Backlog buffer that actually stores propagated commands
3. Command propagation: after each write command, serialize and push to replica write buffers
4. Snapshot streaming: encode current store as RDB and send to new replicas
5. Live command streaming: send write commands to connected replicas in real-time

## Non-Goals for V1
- Multi-master / cluster replication
- Sentinel integration
- Diskless replication (can always go through RDB file)", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.794017436Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T19:18:38.527795780Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T19:18:38.527541499Z", "close_reason": "All sub-tasks complete.  {"id":"frankenredis-xmr8","title":"fr-persist: type-21 consumer-group payload entries_read approximation under-counts","description":"Surfaced by /mock-code-finder pass. encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs was writing group.pending.len() for the entries_read slot in upstream STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 payloads synthesized from in-memory state. Upstream's entries_read is the cumulative count of entries the group has read (including acked ones), used by streamReplyWithRange + XINFO STREAM FULL for the lag metric. pending.len() is the unacked-only count which drops back to zero on every XACK. Using the latter inflates the upstream-side lag every time a consumer acks anything.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:53:35.462633491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:53:43.497656815Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:53:43.495727191Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 433c413: encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs:147 now emits the upstream SCG_INVALID_ENTRIES_READ sentinel (-1 cast to u64, encoded as 0x81 + 8 x 0xFF) for the entries_read slot when fr-persist synthesizes the type-21 payload from in-memory state. Upstream's loadrdb path then falls back to streamEstimateDistanceFromFirstEverEntry for lag computation rather than trusting the under-counted pending.len() approximation. Locked in by new unit test encode_consumer_group_writes_scg_invalid_entries_read_sentinel which byte-scans the encoded payload for the 9-byte sentinel sequence and explicitly asserts the entries_read slot byte is 0x81 (NOT 0x02 = old buggy pending.len()=2). cargo test -p fr-persist 138/138 green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Keep whole-RDB decode strict on trailing bytes and cover U002/I02 with unit tests.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-18T03:13:30.488970740Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-18T05:36:55.668069735Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-18T05:36:55.665053916Z", "close_reason": "Added RDB preamble decode cursor accounting and tests", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["persistence", "phase2c", "upstream-parity"]}
-{"id": "frankenredis-xvlj", "title": "fr-command Lua: redis.error_reply doesn't prepend ERR error code when missing", "description": "Lua's `redis.error_reply("err")` should emit the wire reply '-ERR err\r\n', producing 'ERR err' as displayed by redis-cli. fr instead returns just 'err' (no error code prefix) because crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2463 stores the raw arg verbatim in the {err=...} table.

Upstream script_lua.c::luaRedisErrorReplyCommand prepends '-' when missing, then luaPushErrorBuff parses leading '-CODE [msg]' (split by first space) — if the body has no space, code defaults to 'ERR' and the rest is the message. The table is built as {err = '<CODE> <msg>'}.

Acceptance:
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("err")' 0 → '-ERR err\r\n' on the wire (redis-cli prints 'ERR err')
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("CUSTOM hi")' 0 → '-ERR CUSTOM hi\r\n' (since no leading '-')
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("-CUSTOM hi")' 0 → '-CUSTOM hi\r\n' (leading '-' parsed, code=CUSTOM, msg=hi)
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("-NOMSG")' 0 → '-ERR NOMSG\r\n' (no space after '-CODE' → fallback to default ERR)

Caught by ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701): EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("err")' 0. fr: 'err'. redis: 'ERR err'.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T03:13:40.645188565Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T03:17:14.063856Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T03:17:14.063856Z", "close_reason": "redis.error_reply now mirrors upstream luaRedisErrorReplyCommand + luaPushErrorBuff: prepends '-' when missing, parses '-CODE msg' (defaulting to ERR), trims trailing CRLF. Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4 across 5 cases (err/-CUSTOM/NOMSG/-NOMSG/NOAUTH). Pinned by eval_redis_error_reply_prepends_err_code_when_missing. Shipped in 0ecad05."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-xvlj","title":"fr-command Lua: redis.error_reply doesn't prepend ERR error code when missing","description":"Lua's `redis.error_reply("err")` should emit the wire reply '-ERR err\r\n', producing 'ERR err' as displayed by redis-cli. fr instead returns just 'err' (no error code prefix) because crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2463 stores the raw arg verbatim in the {err=...} table.

Upstream script_lua.c::luaRedisErrorReplyCommand prepends '-' when missing, then luaPushErrorBuff parses leading '-CODE [msg]' (split by first space) — if the body has no space, code defaults to 'ERR' and the rest is the message. The table is built as {err = '<CODE> <msg>'}.

Acceptance:
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("err")' 0 → '-ERR err\r\n' on the wire (redis-cli prints 'ERR err')
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("CUSTOM hi")' 0 → '-ERR CUSTOM hi\r\n' (since no leading '-')
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("-CUSTOM hi")' 0 → '-CUSTOM hi\r\n' (leading '-' parsed, code=CUSTOM, msg=hi)
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("-NOMSG")' 0 → '-ERR NOMSG\r\n' (no space after '-CODE' → fallback to default ERR)

Caught by ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701): EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("err")' 0. fr: 'err'. redis: 'ERR err'.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:13:40.645188565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:13:40.645188565Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "title": "Wire real counters into INFO stats section", "description": "# Wire real counters into INFO stats section

## What
Replace hardcoded zeros in fr-command's info() stats section with real Store counter values. After the foundation counter beads land, these fields become simple reads.

## Fields to fix (fr-command/src/lib.rs, info() function, stats section ~line 8290):
1. expired_keys → store.stat_expired_keys
2. expired_stale_perc → store.stat_expired_stale_perc (or compute from cycle stats)
3. expired_time_cap_reached_count → 0 is acceptable (cap not implemented)
4. expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds → store.stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds
5. evicted_keys → store.stat_evicted_keys
6. total_keys_expired → store.stat_expired_keys (same counter)
7. total_keys_evicted → store.stat_evicted_keys (same counter)
8. keyspace_hits → store.stat_keyspace_hits
9. keyspace_misses → store.stat_keyspace_misses

## Fields in clients section (~line 8198):
10. blocked_clients → store.stat_blocked_clients (populated by server)
11. tracking_clients → 0 (acceptable until CLIENT TRACKING is fully wired)

## Fields in memory section (~line 8218):
12. maxmemory → store.maxmemory_bytes_live (populated by runtime)

## Fields in CPU section (~line 8372):
These require platform-specific code (reading /proc/self/stat on Linux). Lower priority — acceptable to leave as 0 with a comment explaining why.

## Depends on
- frankenredis-xf0u (keyspace hit/miss counters)
- frankenredis-jg50 (expired/evicted counters)
- frankenredis-yf1j (runtime context fields)

## Testing
- Conformance: add INFO stats cases that verify non-zero counters after operations
- Unit test: set + get key, verify keyspace_hits incremented in INFO output

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (info() function stats/clients/memory sections)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:19:16.554565415Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T03:07:43.430353494Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T03:07:43.430099193Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-jg50", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:34.361555156Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-xf0u", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:34.243078331Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-yf1j", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:34.479597319Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-xy1q", "title": "[HIGH] XADD auto-ID + broad stream divergences — fixture clock vs upstream wall clock", "description": "Surfaced by live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime: 48/218 divergences; the majority stem from XADD auto-generated IDs using the fixture now_ms (e.g. '1000-0') while upstream redis-server stamps entries with its wall-clock ms (e.g. '1777004357777-0'). Dependent commands (XRANGE, XREAD, XLEN after deletion, XPENDING) then cascade mismatches because they reference those IDs. Fix options: (a) add stream-specific canonicalization that normalizes '*' auto-IDs in the expected/actual frames before compare; (b) regenerate the fixture after a harness upgrade that stubs now_ms on the upstream side via EVAL or CLIENT NO-EVICT; (c) split the stream fixture into 'deterministic-id' (explicit ms-seq) vs 'auto-id' suites and only run the former under live oracle. Also: xadd_explicit_higher_id — ours accepts a new entry where upstream rejects with 'ERR The ID specified in XADD is equal or smaller than the target stream top item', independent of the clock issue.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T04:21:15.953055702Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T22:38:36.531218175Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T22:38:36.529285866Z", "close_reason": "Already resolved: live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime passes 100% under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1 (fixture-clock canonicalizer on upstream stream IDs landed previously).", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
